name: Scalloped Oysters and Chicken
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 cups Fresh shucked medium-sized oysters OR small oysters 1 3/4 lb (875g) with liquor reserved.
Hot pepper sauce OR a pinch of cayenne pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces.
2. In a skillet, heat 1/4 cup butter over medium-high heat; brown the chicken in batches. Remove the chicken and set aside.
3. Pour off any fat from the skillet. Add wine and bring to a boil, scraping up any brown bits from the pan. Remove from heat.
4. In a saucepan, melt the remaining butter and toss with cracker crumbs. Set aside.
5. Drain oysters, reserving the liquor in a measuring cup. Add enough water to make 3/4 cup.
6. Pat one-third of the cracker crumbs into a greased 13 inch by 9 inch baking dish.
7. Combine chicken with oysters and arrange half of the mixture over the crumbs.
8. Pat half of the remaining crumbs on top; cover with the remaining oyster mixture.
9. Pat the remaining crumbs on top.
10. Combine the oyster liquor, wine mixture, whipping cream, aromatic bitters, Worcestershire sauce, salt, pepper, thyme, and hot pepper sauce (or a pinch of cayenne pepper) in a bowl; pour over the casserole.
11. Bake in a 375 F (190 C) oven for 20 to 25 minutes or until bubbling and heated through. Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.
